title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer is unable to drain water within the allotted time.
fullDescription: The OE error code indicates the washer failed to drain water within the expected timeframe, typically 10 minutes. This can be caused by a clogged drain filter, kinked drain hose, a faulty drain pump, or a blockage in the plumbing system. The washer will stop mid-cycle and display OE on the control panel.

causes.cause: Clogged drain pump filter with debris, coins, or lint
Kinked or clogged drain hose
Faulty drain pump motor
Blockage in household plumbing or standpipe
Defective drain pump impeller

symptoms: Water remains in the drum after the cycle ends
Washer stops mid-cycle and displays OE
Clothes come out soaking wet
Unusual humming or buzzing sound from the drain pump area
Water drains very slowly

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer away from the wall and inspect the drain hose for kinks, bends, or clogs. Ensure the hose is not inserted more than 8 inches into the standpipe. Straighten any kinks and ensure there is an air gap.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small access panel on the front bottom of the washer. Place towels and a shallow container underneath. Turn the drain pump filter counterclockwise slowly to release water. Remove the filter completely and clean off any debris, lint, coins, or hair.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, look inside the pump housing for any foreign objects or debris blocking the impeller. Shine a flashlight inside and try to rotate the impeller by hand. It should spin freely.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the washer from power. Access the drain pump by removing the back or bottom panel. Disconnect the wiring harness from the pump motor and test continuity using a multimeter. The reading should be between 5 and 15 ohms. If out of range, replace the pump.

fixSteps.title: Check household plumbing
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain hose from the standpipe and run water directly into the pipe to verify it drains freely. If water backs up, the blockage is in your plumbing and a plumber is needed.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed and needs replacement, or if the issue is related to household plumbing requiring specialized tools.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer OE error code fix drain

faq.q: Can I still use my washer with an OE error?
faq.a: No, the washer will not complete the cycle. You need to resolve the drain issue before using it again. You can manually drain the water through the drain pump filter at the front bottom of the machine.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain pump filter?
faq.a: LG recommends cleaning the drain pump filter at least once a month to prevent buildup of lint, coins, and debris that can cause the OE error.

faq.q: Why does OE happen only during the spin cycle?
faq.a: The washer attempts to drain water before and during the spin cycle. A partial blockage may allow slow draining during wash but fail during the faster spin drain, triggering OE.

title: Water Inlet Error
shortDescription: The washer is not filling with water or filling too slowly.
fullDescription: The IE error code indicates the washer has not filled with the required amount of water within the allotted time, typically 8 minutes. This can result from closed water supply valves, kinked inlet hoses, clogged inlet screens, low water pressure, or a faulty water inlet valve. The washer will not start or will stop early in the cycle.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets are turned off or not fully open
Clogged inlet hose screens or filters
Kinked or frozen water inlet hoses
Defective water inlet valve
Low household water pressure (below 20 PSI)

symptoms: Washer does not fill with water at all
Washer fills extremely slowly
IE displayed on control panel shortly after starting a cycle
Only hot or cold water fills, not both
Drum remains empty or partially filled

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply valves
fixSteps.instruction: Check both hot and cold water faucets behind the washer. Ensure both are fully turned on (counterclockwise). If you recently moved the washer, they may have been shut off.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply and disconnect both inlet hoses from the back of the washer. Check for kinks, damage, or ice blockage in cold weather. Straighten or replace any damaged hoses.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: With the hoses disconnected, locate the small mesh screens inside the inlet valve connections on the back of the washer. Carefully remove them with needle-nose pliers and rinse them clean under running water. Also check the screens at the faucet end of the hoses.

fixSteps.title: Test water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the washer from power. Remove the top panel to access the inlet valve. Disconnect the wiring harness and test each solenoid coil with a multimeter for continuity. The reading should be between 500 and 1500 ohms. Replace the valve assembly if either coil is open.

whenToCallPro: If the water inlet valve needs replacement or if you suspect low household water pressure that requires a plumber.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer IE error code fix water inlet

faq.q: Why does my LG washer show IE only on cold wash cycles?
faq.a: This indicates the cold water supply is restricted. Check that the cold water faucet is fully open and that the cold-side inlet screen is not clogged with sediment.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause the IE error?
faq.a: Yes. LG washers require a minimum water pressure of 20 PSI. If your home has low water pressure, the washer may not fill in time. Consider having a plumber check your water pressure.

faq.q: How do I reset the IE error code?
faq.a: Fix the underlying water supply issue, then press and hold the Power button for 5 seconds to turn the washer off. Wait 30 seconds and turn it back on. Run a Rinse + Spin cycle to test.

title: Door Lock Error
shortDescription: The washer door is not properly closed or the door lock mechanism has failed.
fullDescription: The dE error code indicates the washer cannot detect that the door is securely closed and locked. This prevents the wash cycle from starting as a safety measure. The issue may be as simple as an obstruction in the door or as complex as a faulty door lock assembly or wiring harness. The dE1 variant indicates a door lock mechanism failure, while dE2 indicates the door switch circuit has an issue.

causes.cause: Door not fully closed or clothing caught in the door seal
Faulty door lock assembly or latch mechanism
Damaged or worn door strike
Broken wiring harness between door lock and control board
Defective main control board

symptoms: Washer will not start a cycle
dE, dE1, or dE2 displayed on the control panel
Door does not click shut or feels loose
Door lock does not engage (no locking sound)
Washer door opens unexpectedly during a cycle

fixSteps.title: Clear door obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the washer door fully and inspect the door seal (gasket) for any clothing, debris, or foreign objects caught between the door and the rubber seal. Remove any obstructions and close the door firmly until you hear a click.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door strike and latch
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door strike (the hook on the door that engages with the lock) for damage, warping, or misalignment. Also check the latch receiver on the washer body. If the strike is bent, try straightening it with pliers.

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer from the electrical outlet for 5 minutes. This resets the control board and can clear false door lock errors. Plug it back in and attempt to start a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Test or replace the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the washer from power. Open the door and peel back the front door gasket to access the door lock assembly, held in place by two screws. Disconnect the wiring harness and test the switch contacts with a multimeter. Replace the assembly if it shows no continuity when activated.

whenToCallPro: If the door lock assembly or wiring harness needs replacement and you are not comfortable disassembling the front panel.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer dE door error code fix

faq.q: What is the difference between dE, dE1, and dE2?
faq.a: dE is a general door error. dE1 specifically indicates the door lock mechanism has failed to engage. dE2 indicates the door switch circuit is not making proper contact. All three point to door-related issues.

faq.q: My door closes but I still get dE. What should I do?
faq.a: The door lock actuator inside the lock assembly may have failed. Try unplugging the washer for 5 minutes to reset. If the error persists, the door lock assembly likely needs replacement.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door lock to use my washer?
faq.a: No. The door lock is a critical safety feature that prevents the door from opening while the drum is spinning at high speed. Bypassing it could result in serious injury or water flooding.

title: Unbalanced Load Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle.
fullDescription: The UE error code (uppercase) indicates the washer attempted to balance the load but was unsuccessful and halted the spin cycle. A lowercase uE means the washer detected an imbalance and is actively attempting to redistribute the load by adding water and tumbling. If the washer cannot correct the imbalance after several attempts, it escalates to an uppercase UE error and stops. Consistently heavy or bulky items on one side of the drum are the most common cause.

causes.cause: Unevenly distributed laundry load (heavy items on one side)
Washing a single heavy item like a blanket or comforter
Washer not level on the floor
Worn or broken suspension rods or shock absorbers
Damaged drum bearings causing excessive vibration

symptoms: Washer stops during the spin cycle
uE displayed during spinning attempts, then UE if unsuccessful
Excessive vibration or shaking during spin
Loud banging or thumping noise during spin cycle
Clothes remain wet at the end of the cycle

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Pause the cycle and open the door. Redistribute the laundry evenly around the drum. If washing a single heavy item like a blanket, add a few towels to help balance the load. Close the door and restart the spin cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check load size
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure you are not overloading or underloading the washer. A load that is too small (1-2 items) or too large can cause imbalance. The drum should be about 3/4 full for optimal balance.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Place a bubble level on top of the washer. Adjust the leveling feet by turning them clockwise to lower or counterclockwise to raise. The washer should be perfectly level front-to-back and side-to-side. Tighten the lock nuts against the washer body once level.

fixSteps.title: Inspect suspension components
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel by unscrewing the rear screws and sliding it back. Inspect the four suspension rods (front-load) or springs (top-load) for damage, stretching, or disconnection. Replace any worn or broken suspension components.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after redistributing loads and leveling the washer, the suspension system or drum bearings may need professional diagnosis and repair.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer UE unbalanced error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between uE and UE?
faq.a: Lowercase uE means the washer detected an imbalance and is trying to correct it automatically. Uppercase UE means the washer tried and failed to balance the load and has stopped. Only UE requires your intervention.

faq.q: Why does my washer always show UE with bed sheets?
faq.a: Bed sheets and large items tend to ball up on one side of the drum. Try washing sheets separately, adding a few towels for balance, and not overloading the drum.

faq.q: Can an unlevel washer cause UE errors?
faq.a: Yes, an unlevel washer is a very common cause of recurring UE errors. The drum cannot distribute weight evenly if the machine itself is tilted. Always verify the washer is level on all four feet.

title: Child Lock Activated
shortDescription: The child lock feature is engaged, preventing button presses.
fullDescription: CL is not an error code but a status indicator showing the child lock feature is active. When CL is displayed, all buttons on the control panel are disabled except the Power button. This prevents children from accidentally changing settings or starting the washer. The tCL variant may appear on some models during the child lock activation process.

causes.cause: Child lock was intentionally activated by pressing and holding the designated buttons
Child lock was accidentally activated by a child or while cleaning the panel
Control board glitch causing false child lock activation
Sticky or malfunctioning control panel buttons

symptoms: CL displayed on the control panel
No buttons respond when pressed except Power
Cannot start, stop, or change wash cycles
Beeping sound when pressing buttons

fixSteps.title: Deactivate child lock
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Child Lock button (or the designated button combination, often Temp and Options or Soil Level and Pre Wash) for 3-5 seconds until CL disappears from the display and the washer beeps.

fixSteps.title: Check your model's specific button combo
fixSteps.instruction: If the above does not work, check the control panel label for a small lock icon indicating which buttons to hold. On some models, it is the Rinse and Spin buttons held together for 3 seconds.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the washer
fixSteps.instruction: If child lock will not deactivate, turn the washer off using the Power button. Unplug the washer from the wall outlet for 1 minute. Plug it back in and turn it on. Try deactivating the child lock again.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control panel
fixSteps.instruction: If the child lock keeps engaging on its own, the control panel membrane buttons may be malfunctioning. Disconnect power, remove the top panel, and inspect the control panel ribbon cable connection. Clean and reseat the connection.

whenToCallPro: Only if the child lock will not deactivate after power cycling and attempting all button combinations, which may indicate a control panel malfunction.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer CL child lock deactivate

faq.q: Is CL an error code?
faq.a: No, CL stands for Child Lock and is a feature, not an error. It means the control panel buttons are locked to prevent accidental changes.

faq.q: Which buttons do I hold to turn off CL?
faq.a: It varies by model. Common combinations are: Temp + Options held for 3 seconds, or the dedicated Child Lock button if your model has one. Check the control panel for a small lock icon near the button combination.

faq.q: Why does CL keep turning on by itself?
faq.a: This could indicate sticky buttons or a control panel issue. Try cleaning the control panel surface. If it persists, the control panel membrane may need replacement.

title: Locked Motor Error
shortDescription: The washer motor is locked or unable to rotate the drum.
fullDescription: The LE error code indicates the drive motor is unable to rotate the wash drum. This can be caused by an overloaded washer, a jammed drum, a faulty rotor position sensor (hall sensor), problems with the stator or rotor assembly, or a main control board failure. LE1 specifically indicates a low-voltage condition affecting the motor. The washer will stop mid-cycle when this error occurs.

causes.cause: Washer is overloaded beyond its capacity
Faulty rotor position sensor (hall sensor)
Loose or damaged stator wiring connections
Worn or broken rotor assembly magnets
Defective main control board

symptoms: LE or LE1 displayed on the control panel
Drum does not spin or agitate
Washer hums but drum does not move
Error appears shortly after starting a cycle
Intermittent motor operation with stopping and starting

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: An overloaded drum puts excessive strain on the motor. Remove some items from the drum to reduce the load to about 3/4 full. Reset the washer by unplugging it for 1 minute and try again.

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer from the wall outlet for 10 minutes. This allows the motor control board to fully reset. Plug it back in and run an empty Rinse + Spin cycle to test. If LE does not appear on an empty load, overloading was the issue.

fixSteps.title: Check the rotor position sensor (hall sensor)
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the rear panel of the washer to access the motor assembly. Locate the small sensor mounted on the stator assembly near the rotor shaft. Disconnect its wiring harness and test for about 11-13 ohms resistance between the sensor wires using a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the stator and rotor
fixSteps.instruction: With the rear panel removed, remove the rotor bolt (typically 14mm) and pull the rotor off the shaft. Inspect the stator windings for burn marks or damage. Check the rotor magnets for cracks or detachment. Also verify the stator wiring harness is securely connected to the control board.

whenToCallPro: If the stator, rotor, or main control board need replacement. These are major components that require technical knowledge to diagnose and install.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer LE error code motor fix

faq.q: What does LE1 mean versus LE?
faq.a: LE indicates the motor is locked or unable to turn. LE1 specifically indicates a low-voltage condition. LE1 can be caused by loose wiring or a power supply issue in addition to motor problems.

faq.q: Can overloading really cause LE?
faq.a: Yes, overloading is the most common cause. The motor cannot generate enough torque to spin an extremely heavy load. Simply reducing the load size and resetting the washer resolves the issue in most cases.

faq.q: Is the LE error expensive to fix?
faq.a: It depends on the cause. If it is an overload issue, it costs nothing. A hall sensor replacement is about $15 in parts. However, a stator or rotor replacement can cost $80-150 for the part alone.

title: Current Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an electrical overcurrent condition in the motor.
fullDescription: The CE error code indicates the main control board has detected an overcurrent condition from the drive motor. This means the motor is drawing more electrical current than expected, which could damage the control board if left unchecked. Common causes include a shorted stator winding, a defective main control board, or a seized drum bearing placing excessive load on the motor.

causes.cause: Shorted stator windings causing excessive current draw
Defective main control board power transistors
Seized or worn drum bearings increasing motor load
Loose wiring connections between stator and control board

symptoms: CE displayed on the control panel
Washer stops during operation
Motor runs briefly then shuts down
Possible burning smell from the motor area

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer from the wall outlet for 15 minutes to allow the motor and control board to cool down and reset. Plug it back in and try running an empty Rinse + Spin cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the rear panel to access the motor assembly. Inspect all wiring connections between the stator assembly and the main control board. Look for loose, burnt, or corroded connectors. Reseat all connections firmly.

fixSteps.title: Test the stator windings
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the stator wiring harness from the control board. Using a multimeter, measure the resistance between each pair of stator wires. All three pairs should read between 5 and 9 ohms. If any pair is significantly different or reads zero, the stator is shorted and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check drum bearings
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer empty and unplugged, try spinning the drum by hand. It should rotate smoothly with minimal resistance. If you feel grinding, roughness, or excessive resistance, the drum bearings may be seized and need professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: CE errors typically require professional diagnosis as they involve the motor or control board. Call a pro if the error persists after power cycling and checking wiring connections.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer CE current error fix motor

faq.q: Is CE a serious error?
faq.a: Yes, CE indicates an overcurrent condition which can damage the main control board if not addressed. Do not continue to use the washer until the issue is resolved.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause the CE error?
faq.a: A power surge can potentially damage the control board components that monitor motor current, leading to a CE error. Using a surge protector is recommended.

faq.q: How much does it cost to fix a CE error?
faq.a: Costs vary significantly. If it is a wiring issue, it may be free. A stator replacement costs $80-150, while a control board replacement is $150-300 for the part. Professional labor adds $100-200.

title: Water Level Pressure Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is malfunctioning or providing incorrect readings.
fullDescription: The PE error code indicates the washer's water level pressure sensor (also called the pressure switch or frequency sensor) is not providing valid signals to the main control board. This sensor determines how much water is in the drum and controls filling and draining. A faulty sensor can cause the washer to overfill, underfill, or fail to start.

causes.cause: Defective water level pressure sensor
Kinked, disconnected, or cracked pressure hose
Loose wiring connection to the pressure sensor
Clogged pressure hose or air dome chamber

symptoms: PE displayed on the control panel
Washer will not start filling with water
Washer overfills with water
Incorrect water levels during wash cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes and plug it back in. Sometimes the PE error is triggered by a momentary sensor glitch that a reset will clear.

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel by unscrewing the rear screws and sliding it backward. Locate the pressure sensor (a small round disc usually near the top of the washer). Follow the small rubber hose from the sensor down to the outer tub. Ensure the hose is not kinked, cracked, or disconnected at either end.

fixSteps.title: Clear the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure hose from the sensor. Blow gently into the hose to clear any blockages. You should feel no resistance. Also check the air dome fitting on the outer tub for debris. Reconnect the hose securely.

fixSteps.title: Test or replace the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the wiring harness from the pressure sensor. Test the sensor with a multimeter by measuring resistance while blowing gently into the hose port. The resistance should change as pressure increases. If the readings are erratic or do not change, replace the sensor.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable removing the top panel or diagnosing the pressure sensor circuit. Also if the main control board may be at fault.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer PE error code pressure sensor fix

faq.q: What does the water level pressure sensor do?
faq.a: The sensor measures the water level in the drum by detecting air pressure changes in a hose connected to the outer tub. As water rises, it compresses air in the hose, and the sensor reports the pressure to the control board.

faq.q: Can I wash clothes with a PE error?
faq.a: No, the washer will not operate correctly without a functioning pressure sensor. It may overfill or not fill at all, both of which are problematic.

faq.q: Is the PE error related to water pressure?
faq.a: No, PE refers to the internal water level pressure sensor, not your household water pressure. Low household water pressure would trigger an IE error instead.

title: Power Failure
shortDescription: The washer experienced a power interruption during a cycle.
fullDescription: The PF error code indicates a power failure or fluctuation occurred while the washer was running a cycle. This is not a machine malfunction but an indication that electrical power was interrupted. Common causes include power outages, loose wall outlet connections, tripped circuit breakers, or voltage fluctuations. The washer remembers where it was in the cycle and can resume after the power is restored.

causes.cause: Power outage or brownout during the wash cycle
Loose wall outlet or power cord connection
Tripped circuit breaker or blown fuse
Faulty electrical outlet or wiring

symptoms: PF displayed on the control panel after power is restored
Washer stopped mid-cycle
All indicator lights flash or reset
Cycle does not resume automatically

fixSteps.title: Press Start/Pause to resume
fixSteps.instruction: Simply press the Start/Pause button to resume the cycle from where it stopped. The washer remembers its position in the cycle and will continue.

fixSteps.title: Check the power source
fixSteps.instruction: If PF recurs, verify the power cord is firmly plugged into the wall outlet. Test the outlet with another device to ensure it is working. Check your circuit breaker panel for tripped breakers.

fixSteps.title: Avoid extension cords
fixSteps.instruction: LG washers should be plugged directly into a grounded wall outlet. Extension cords and power strips can cause voltage drops that trigger PF. Remove any extension cord and plug directly into the wall.

fixSteps.title: Check for electrical issues
fixSteps.instruction: If PF occurs repeatedly without any known power outages, the wall outlet wiring may be loose or faulty. Have a licensed electrician inspect the outlet and the dedicated circuit serving the washer.

whenToCallPro: If PF errors occur repeatedly without any known power outages, have an electrician inspect the outlet and circuit. Consider using a surge protector rated for appliances.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer PF power failure error fix

faq.q: Do I need to restart the entire cycle after PF?
faq.a: No, pressing Start/Pause will resume the cycle from where it was interrupted. If you prefer to start fresh, press Power to turn the washer off, then select a new cycle.

faq.q: Can PF damage my washer?
faq.a: Occasional power failures will not damage the washer. However, frequent power surges can damage the main control board over time. Consider using an appliance surge protector.

faq.q: Should I use a surge protector with my LG washer?
faq.a: Yes, an appliance-grade surge protector is recommended to protect the sensitive electronic components. Avoid standard power strips, which may not handle the washer's current draw.

title: Thermistor Error (Heating Sensor)
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor (thermistor) has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The tE error code indicates the washer's thermistor (water temperature sensor) is reporting abnormal readings or has failed. The thermistor monitors the water temperature during wash cycles and communicates with the control board to regulate heating. A faulty thermistor can prevent the washer from operating or cause incorrect water temperatures.

causes.cause: Defective thermistor sensor
Loose or disconnected thermistor wiring
Short circuit in the thermistor wiring harness
Faulty main control board thermistor input circuit

symptoms: tE displayed on the control panel
Washer will not start or stops mid-cycle
Water temperature is not correct for the selected setting
Hot wash cycles do not heat properly

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes and plug it back in. Run a cold wash cycle to see if the error clears. If the error only appears on warm or hot cycles, the thermistor is likely the issue.

fixSteps.title: Locate the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. The thermistor is typically located at the bottom of the outer tub or near the heater element. Remove the rear or bottom panel to access it. It is a small two-wire sensor.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wiring harness. Using a multimeter, measure the resistance of the thermistor. At room temperature (about 77F/25C), it should read approximately 50,000 ohms (50k). If it reads 0 ohms (shorted) or infinite (open), replace it.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: If the thermistor is faulty, remove it from its housing (usually a clip or friction fit in the tub). Install the new thermistor in the same position and reconnect the wiring harness. Reassemble the panel and test.

whenToCallPro: If you are uncomfortable accessing the thermistor location or if the wiring harness has multiple damaged wires.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer tE error thermistor fix

faq.q: Can I still wash clothes with a tE error?
faq.a: The washer may not allow you to start any cycle with a tE error active. Even if it does start, water temperature will not be regulated correctly.

faq.q: What is a thermistor?
faq.a: A thermistor is a type of temperature sensor that changes its electrical resistance based on temperature. The washer uses it to monitor water temperature and control the heating element.

faq.q: Is the thermistor expensive to replace?
faq.a: No, the thermistor itself is one of the least expensive washer parts, typically $8-20. The main cost for professional repair is labor.

title: Overflow Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected that the water level exceeds the maximum allowed level.
fullDescription: The FE error code indicates the washer has detected a water overflow condition. Water is filling beyond the safe level. This is a critical safety error that triggers an automatic drain cycle. Causes include a stuck-open water inlet valve, a faulty water level pressure sensor, or excessive suds causing false sensor readings.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve stuck in the open position
Faulty water level pressure sensor giving incorrect readings
Excessive detergent creating too many suds
Main control board not sending proper valve shutoff signal

symptoms: FE displayed on the control panel
Water continues to fill even when the washer is paused or off
Water overflows from the washer
Washer initiates an emergency drain cycle

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If water is actively overflowing or the washer is overfilling, turn off both hot and cold water supply valves behind the washer immediately. This stops water from entering the machine.

fixSteps.title: Check for excess suds
fixSteps.instruction: If you used too much detergent or non-HE detergent, excess suds can cause false overflow readings. Run a Rinse + Spin cycle with no detergent. Use only HE (High Efficiency) detergent and the recommended amount.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With water supply on and washer powered off, observe whether water continues to trickle into the drum. If it does, the inlet valve is stuck open and needs replacement. Water should completely stop flowing when the washer is off.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water supply. Remove the top panel and locate the inlet valve at the rear of the washer where the hoses connect. Disconnect the wiring harness and water lines. Remove the mounting screws and replace the valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: If water continues to fill when the washer is off, this indicates a stuck inlet valve which should be replaced promptly to prevent water damage.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer FE overflow error fix inlet valve

faq.q: Is FE dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, an overflow condition can cause water damage to your home. Immediately turn off the water supply valves if you see FE or notice the washer overfilling.

faq.q: Can using too much detergent cause FE?
faq.a: Yes, excessive suds from too much detergent or non-HE detergent can trigger the FE error. Always use HE detergent and follow the dosing instructions.

faq.q: Why does my washer still fill with water when it is off?
faq.a: If water enters the drum while the washer is powered off, the water inlet valve is stuck open. This requires immediate valve replacement to prevent flooding.

title: Leak Sensor Error (Aqua Lock)
shortDescription: The washer's leak detection system has detected water in the base pan.
fullDescription: The AE error code indicates the washer has detected water leaking into the base pan underneath the drum. LG washers have a leak sensor in the bottom tray that triggers this error to prevent water damage. The leak could come from loose hose connections, a torn door boot seal, a cracked outer tub, or a leaking pump.

causes.cause: Loose or damaged water hose connections
Torn or damaged door boot seal (gasket)
Leaking drain pump seal
Cracked outer tub or tub seal failure

symptoms: AE displayed on the control panel
Water visible on the floor around the washer
Washer stops mid-cycle and will not restart
Musty smell from water sitting in the base

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply and unplug the washer. Pull the washer away from the wall and inspect underneath and behind it for signs of water. Identify where the water is coming from.

fixSteps.title: Inspect hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Check all water hose connections at the back of the washer and at the wall faucets. Tighten any loose connections by hand and then a quarter turn with pliers. Check the drain hose connection to the pump and the standpipe.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door boot seal
fixSteps.instruction: Open the washer door and carefully examine the entire rubber door boot seal for tears, holes, or debris that could prevent proper sealing. Run your fingers along the inner and outer lips of the seal. Also check for foreign objects caught between the door glass and the seal.

fixSteps.title: Drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the washer back to access the base. Use towels to soak up any water in the base pan. Check if the leak sensor float is stuck in the up position. Clean the sensor and base pan, then test. If the error was caused by a one-time event (like a hose loosening), it may not recur.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is coming from the outer tub or an internal seal that requires major disassembly. Also if you cannot identify the source of the leak.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer AE leak error code fix

faq.q: Can I continue using the washer with AE?
faq.a: No, the AE error indicates a water leak which can cause damage to your floors and potentially create electrical hazards. Fix the leak before using the washer.

faq.q: How do I reset the AE error after fixing the leak?
faq.a: After fixing the leak, dry out the base pan and ensure the leak sensor float moves freely. Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then plug it back in. The error should clear once the sensor detects no water.

faq.q: Is it normal for the base pan to have a little water?
faq.a: No, the base pan should be completely dry during normal operation. Any water in the base pan indicates a leak that needs to be addressed.

title: EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The washer's control board memory chip has a read/write error.
fullDescription: The EE error code indicates a problem with the EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory) chip on the main control board. This chip stores the washer's operating parameters and cycle data. When the control board cannot read from or write to the EEPROM, it displays this error. This is typically a control board failure.

causes.cause: EEPROM chip failure on the main control board
Power surge or electrical spike corrupting the EEPROM data
Loose or corroded connection on the control board
Manufacturing defect in the control board

symptoms: EE displayed on the control panel
Washer will not start any cycle
Control panel may be unresponsive
Previously saved custom cycles are lost

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer from the wall outlet for 15 minutes. This extended reset can sometimes clear corrupted EEPROM data and allow the board to reinitialize. Plug it back in and attempt to start a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Try a hard reset
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer plugged in, press and hold the Start/Pause button for 5 seconds. Then unplug the washer, wait 30 seconds, and plug it back in. Press Power and try to start a basic cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check control board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel to access the main control board. Inspect all ribbon cables and wiring harness connections to the board. Reseat any loose connections. Look for signs of corrosion or burn marks on the board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the main control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the EE error persists after resets and connection checks, the main control board needs replacement. Disconnect all wiring harnesses from the old board, noting their positions. Remove the mounting screws and install the new board. Reconnect all harnesses.

whenToCallPro: EE errors almost always require control board replacement. Unless you are comfortable with electronics repair, a professional is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer EE error EEPROM control board fix

faq.q: Can a power outage cause the EE error?
faq.a: Yes, power surges during an outage can corrupt the EEPROM data. This is why using an appliance surge protector is recommended.

faq.q: Is the EE error always a control board replacement?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. A power cycle may temporarily clear the error, but if the EEPROM chip itself is damaged, the error will return and the board needs replacement.

faq.q: How long do LG washer control boards last?
faq.a: Control boards typically last 8-12 years under normal conditions. Power surges, moisture exposure, and electrical issues can shorten their lifespan.

title: Excessive Suds Detected
shortDescription: The washer has detected too many suds in the drum.
fullDescription: The Sud (or Sd) code indicates the washer has detected excessive suds during the wash cycle. Too many suds reduce cleaning effectiveness, can cause leaks, and may damage the washer. The washer will pause the cycle and allow the suds to dissipate before continuing. This is almost always caused by using too much detergent or using non-HE detergent in an HE washer.

causes.cause: Using too much detergent
Using non-HE (non-High Efficiency) detergent
Using liquid detergent designed for hand washing
Clogged drain causing suds to accumulate

symptoms: Sud displayed on the control panel
Visible foam or suds leaking from the door seal
Wash cycle pauses frequently
Extended cycle times as washer waits for suds to dissipate

fixSteps.title: Let the cycle continue
fixSteps.instruction: The washer will pause to allow suds to dissipate, then continue the cycle. This process may take extra time. Allow the washer to complete the cycle on its own.

fixSteps.title: Run a rinse cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If suds persist, run a Rinse + Spin cycle with no detergent to flush out excess suds. You may need to run this 2-3 times for heavy suds buildup.

fixSteps.title: Switch to HE detergent
fixSteps.instruction: Only use detergent marked with the HE (High Efficiency) symbol. Use the amount recommended on the package, typically 1-2 tablespoons for a normal load. For soft water, use even less.

fixSteps.title: Clean the washer drum
fixSteps.instruction: Run LG's Tub Clean cycle (or a hot water cycle with no clothes) once a month to prevent detergent buildup. Add a washer cleaning tablet or 1 cup of white vinegar to the empty drum before running the cycle.

whenToCallPro: The Sud error rarely requires professional service. If it persists with correct detergent usage, there may be a drain issue that needs investigation.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer Sud suds error fix HE detergent

faq.q: Is Sud an error code?
faq.a: Sud is technically a status code rather than an error. It indicates the washer detected excessive suds and is pausing to let them dissipate. It is a protective measure, not a malfunction.

faq.q: How much HE detergent should I use?
faq.a: For most loads, use 1-2 tablespoons of HE detergent. For heavily soiled loads, use up to the maximum line on the detergent cup. Less is more with HE washers.

faq.q: Can I use regular detergent in my LG washer?
faq.a: No, LG front-load and HE top-load washers require HE detergent. Regular detergent produces too many suds which can cause the Sud error, reduce cleaning performance, and potentially damage the machine.

title: Cool Down Cycle Active
shortDescription: The washer is in the cool-down phase after a dryer cycle (washer-dryer combos).
fullDescription: The Cd code appears on LG washer-dryer combination units and indicates the dryer portion has completed and the cool-down tumbling phase is active. This is a normal status indicator, not an error. The drum continues to tumble without heat to prevent wrinkles and cool the clothes. The door will unlock once the cool-down is complete and the drum temperature is safe.

causes.cause: Normal cool-down period after drying cycle completion
Temperature sensor requires more cool-down time in hot environments
Defective thermistor reading high temperatures incorrectly (extended Cd)
Faulty cooling fan not lowering drum temperature (extended Cd)

symptoms: Cd displayed on the control panel after a dry cycle
Drum continues to tumble slowly
Door remains locked during cool-down
No heat is produced during this phase

fixSteps.title: Wait for cool-down to complete
fixSteps.instruction: The Cd phase typically lasts 15-30 minutes. Allow it to complete naturally. The door will unlock automatically when the drum temperature is safe.

fixSteps.title: Check for extended cool-down
fixSteps.instruction: If Cd continues for more than 45 minutes, the unit may have a cooling issue. Check that the ventilation area is not blocked and the unit has adequate clearance on all sides (at least 4 inches).

fixSteps.title: Press Pause to end early
fixSteps.instruction: If you need to end the cool-down early, press the Start/Pause button. Wait a few minutes for the door lock to disengage. Note that clothes may be warm or hot when you open the door.

fixSteps.title: Check the exhaust system
fixSteps.instruction: If Cd takes excessively long consistently, the dryer exhaust vent may be clogged, preventing proper cooling. Disconnect the exhaust hose and check for lint buildup. Clean the vent path thoroughly.

whenToCallPro: Only if the Cd phase consistently lasts over 45 minutes, which may indicate a ventilation or sensor problem.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer dryer combo Cd cool down code

faq.q: Is Cd an error code?
faq.a: No, Cd stands for Cool Down and is a normal status indicator on LG washer-dryer combos. It means the dryer has finished and the unit is cooling the clothes.

faq.q: Can I skip the Cd cool-down phase?
faq.a: You can press Start/Pause to end it early, but be careful opening the door as the clothes and drum may still be hot.

faq.q: Why does Cd last so long?
faq.a: The cool-down typically lasts 15-30 minutes. It may take longer if the room is hot, the vent is partially clogged, or a large load was dried at high heat.

title: Tub Clean Reminder
shortDescription: The washer is reminding you to run a Tub Clean cycle.
fullDescription: The tCL code is a maintenance reminder, not an error. It appears after approximately 30 wash cycles to remind you to run the Tub Clean cycle. This cycle uses hot water and high spin speeds to clean the inner and outer tub surfaces, removing detergent residue, mildew, and bacteria buildup. Running tub clean regularly helps prevent odors and maintains washer performance.

causes.cause: 30 wash cycles have been completed since the last Tub Clean cycle
Tub Clean cycle was never run on the machine
Previous Tub Clean cycle was interrupted or incomplete
Control board counter was not reset after tub cleaning

symptoms: tCL flashes on the display when the washer is turned on
Musty or mildew odor from the washer
Visible residue or discoloration inside the drum
Dark spots on the door seal

fixSteps.title: Prepare for Tub Clean
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all clothes and items from the drum. Leave the drum completely empty. Do not add any regular detergent.

fixSteps.title: Add a cleaning agent
fixSteps.instruction: Place a washer cleaning tablet (like Affresh) in the drum, or pour 1 cup of liquid chlorine bleach into the detergent dispenser. Do not use both at the same time.

fixSteps.title: Run the Tub Clean cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Select the Tub Clean cycle on the control panel (it is a dedicated cycle option). Press Start. The cycle uses hot water and extra rinsing to clean the interior. It takes approximately 1 hour.

fixSteps.title: Dry the drum and seal
fixSteps.instruction: After the Tub Clean cycle completes, wipe the inside of the door and the rubber door seal with a dry cloth. Leave the washer door slightly open to allow air circulation and prevent moisture buildup.

whenToCallPro: The tCL reminder never requires professional service. However, if your washer has severe mold or odor problems, a professional deep cleaning may be beneficial.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer tCL tub clean cycle how to

faq.q: Is tCL an error code?
faq.a: No, tCL is a maintenance reminder telling you it is time to run the Tub Clean cycle. It is a helpful feature, not an indication of a problem.

faq.q: Can I ignore the tCL reminder?
faq.a: You can dismiss it and wash normally, but it is strongly recommended to run Tub Clean regularly to prevent odor, mold, and residue buildup. The reminder will reappear.

faq.q: How often should I run Tub Clean?
faq.a: LG recommends running the Tub Clean cycle once a month or approximately every 30 wash cycles, which is when the tCL reminder appears.

title: Dryer Heater Error (Combo Units)
shortDescription: The heater in a washer-dryer combo unit has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The dHE error code appears on LG washer-dryer combination units and indicates the dryer heater is not functioning correctly. The heater may not be producing heat, overheating, or the control board cannot communicate with the heater circuit. This prevents the dryer function from working while the washer function may still operate normally.

causes.cause: Faulty dryer heater element (burned out or open circuit)
Defective thermal fuse or thermostat
Clogged exhaust vent causing overheating and safety shutoff
Wiring harness damage between heater and control board

symptoms: dHE displayed during or before a dry cycle
Clothes not drying (tumbling but no heat)
Dryer function stops mid-cycle
Washer function still works normally

fixSteps.title: Check the exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: A clogged exhaust vent is the most common and simplest cause. Disconnect the exhaust hose from the back of the unit and check for lint buildup. Clean the entire vent path from the unit to the exterior wall vent.

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the combo unit for 10 minutes to allow the heater safety devices to reset. Plug it back in and try a short dry cycle. If the vent was clogged, this may resolve the issue.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermal fuse and thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heater assembly by removing the back panel. Locate the thermal fuse and high-limit thermostat on the heater housing. Test each for continuity with a multimeter. Replace any that show no continuity.

fixSteps.title: Test the heater element
fixSteps.instruction: With the heater assembly accessible, disconnect the wiring and test the heater element for continuity. It should read between 10 and 30 ohms. Also test for continuity between each terminal and the heater housing (ground). Any continuity to ground means the element is shorted and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the heater element needs replacement or if you are not comfortable working with high-voltage components.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer dryer combo dHE heater error fix

faq.q: Can I still wash clothes with a dHE error?
faq.a: Yes, the washer function typically still works with a dHE error since it only affects the dryer heater. However, you cannot use the drying function until the issue is resolved.

faq.q: Is a clogged vent really the cause?
faq.a: A clogged vent is the most common cause of dryer heater failures. The restricted airflow causes overheating, which trips the thermal fuse. Always check and clean the vent first.

faq.q: How long does a heater element last?
faq.a: A dryer heater element typically lasts 8-12 years. A clogged vent can significantly shorten this lifespan by causing the element to overheat repeatedly.

title: Motor Current Detection Error
shortDescription: The control board is unable to properly sense the motor current.
fullDescription: The E3 error code indicates the main control board cannot properly detect or measure the current flowing to the drive motor. This could be a sensor issue on the control board, a wiring problem, or an issue with the motor itself. The washer cannot safely operate without current detection as it prevents overcurrent protection.

causes.cause: Faulty current sensor on the main control board
Loose motor wiring connections
Damaged motor winding affecting current draw patterns
Main control board failure

symptoms: E3 displayed on the control panel
Motor does not run or runs erratically
Washer will not start a cycle
Motor stops and starts repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes. This extended reset allows all capacitors to discharge and can clear transient sensor errors. Plug it back in and try starting a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check motor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the rear panel. Locate the motor wiring harness connections at the stator and at the control board. Disconnect and reconnect each connector firmly, checking for corrosion or burn marks on the pins.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor stator
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the stator wiring from the control board. Measure resistance between each pair of the three stator wires. All should read 5-9 ohms. Unequal readings indicate a motor fault.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If motor tests are normal and connections are good, the control board's current sensing circuit has likely failed. Replace the main control board following proper procedures for wiring harness connections.

whenToCallPro: E3 errors usually require professional diagnosis to determine whether the motor or control board is at fault. Recommended for most homeowners.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer E3 error code motor fix

faq.q: Is E3 common on LG washers?
faq.a: E3 is relatively uncommon compared to codes like OE or UE. It typically indicates a more serious control board or motor issue.

faq.q: Can a reset fix E3?
faq.a: Sometimes. If the error was caused by a transient electrical issue, a 10-minute unplugged reset can clear it. If it returns, there is a hardware fault.

faq.q: How much does E3 cost to fix?
faq.a: E3 repairs typically cost $150-500 depending on whether the motor or control board needs replacement. Professional diagnosis is recommended to avoid replacing the wrong part.

title: Clutch Error
shortDescription: The washer's clutch mechanism or shifting assembly has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The E6 error code indicates a problem with the washer's clutch or shifting mechanism. On top-load LG washers, the clutch engages to shift between wash and spin modes. When the control board detects the clutch is not engaging properly or the shift actuator is not responding, it displays E6. This prevents the washer from transitioning between agitation and spin.

causes.cause: Faulty shift actuator motor
Worn clutch assembly
Loose or disconnected shift actuator wiring
Control board unable to send shift signal

symptoms: E6 displayed on the control panel
Washer agitates but will not spin
Washer will not transition between cycles
Grinding or clicking noise from under the machine

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes and plug it back in. Sometimes the shift actuator gets stuck in a position that a reset can clear. Try running a Drain + Spin cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check shift actuator wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Tilt the washer back and support it safely. Locate the shift actuator on the bottom of the transmission housing. Check that its wiring harness is securely connected.

fixSteps.title: Test the shift actuator
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the shift actuator wiring. Using a multimeter, measure the resistance of the actuator motor. It should read between 1000 and 2000 ohms. If open or shorted, replace the actuator.

fixSteps.title: Replace the shift actuator
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the two or three mounting bolts securing the shift actuator to the gearcase. Disconnect the wiring and remove the old actuator. Install the new actuator, reconnect wiring, and secure the mounting bolts.

whenToCallPro: If the clutch assembly itself needs replacement, as this is a more involved repair requiring transmission access.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer E6 error clutch shift actuator fix

faq.q: Is E6 only on top-load washers?
faq.a: E6 is primarily seen on LG top-load washers that use a clutch/shift mechanism. Front-load washers use a different motor design and do not have a clutch assembly.

faq.q: What does the shift actuator do?
faq.a: The shift actuator is an electric motor that mechanically shifts the washer's transmission between agitation mode and spin mode. It engages the clutch to change the drum's motion.

faq.q: Can I replace the shift actuator myself?
faq.a: Yes, it is one of the more accessible repairs on top-load washers. The actuator is mounted on the bottom of the gearcase and requires only basic tools to replace.

title: Locked Rotor - Motor Seized
shortDescription: The motor rotor is locked and unable to rotate.
fullDescription: The Lr error code means the washer's rotor (the rotating part of the direct drive motor) is locked and cannot turn. This is similar to the LE error but specifically indicates the rotor assembly has physically seized. Common causes include a foreign object jammed between the rotor and stator, bearing failure, or a severely overloaded drum.

causes.cause: Foreign object lodged between rotor and stator
Severely overloaded drum exceeding motor capacity
Seized drum bearings preventing rotation
Broken rotor magnets physically blocking rotation

symptoms: Lr displayed on the control panel
Drum does not rotate at all
Motor hums but nothing moves
Possible grinding noise when motor attempts to turn

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the washer and remove about half the laundry. An extremely heavy load can prevent the motor from starting. Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, plug back in, and try again with the lighter load.

fixSteps.title: Check for drum obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the drum by hand. If it does not rotate freely or you feel resistance, something may be jammed. Remove all clothes and check for items caught between the drum and tub. Look between the drum and door seal.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the rotor area
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and remove the rear panel. Inspect the gap between the rotor and stator for any foreign objects (underwire from bras, coins, screws). Also check for broken rotor magnets.

fixSteps.title: Check drum bearings
fixSteps.instruction: With the rear panel off and rotor removed, try spinning the drum shaft by hand. It should rotate smoothly. If you feel grinding or heavy resistance, the drum bearings have failed and need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If drum bearings have failed, as this is a major repair requiring disassembly of the inner and outer tub. Also if you find broken rotor magnets.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer Lr locked rotor error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between LE and Lr?
faq.a: LE means the motor is locked (could be electrical or mechanical). Lr specifically means the rotor is physically locked and cannot turn. Lr is typically a more mechanical issue.

faq.q: Can a small item cause Lr?
faq.a: Yes, something as small as an underwire from a bra or a coin can wedge between the rotor and stator, physically preventing rotation.

faq.q: Is bearing replacement worth it on an older washer?
faq.a: Bearing replacement costs $200-500 professionally. If the washer is over 8-10 years old, compare the repair cost to the price of a new washer to make an informed decision.

title: Drain Timeout During Spin
shortDescription: The washer could not drain enough water to begin the spin cycle.
fullDescription: The dr error code indicates the washer was unable to drain sufficient water to safely begin the high-speed spin cycle. While similar to OE, the dr code specifically occurs when the drain times out at the transition to spin. The washer requires a minimum water level before it will engage the high-speed spin for safety reasons.

causes.cause: Clogged drain pump filter restricting flow
Partially blocked drain hose
Drain pump running at reduced capacity
Drain standpipe height exceeds maximum (over 96 inches)

symptoms: dr displayed on the control panel during spin cycle
Washer completes wash but stops before spin
Water drains slowly during the cycle
Standing water remains after attempted spin

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the access panel at the front bottom of the washer. Place towels and a pan beneath it. Slowly turn the drain pump filter counterclockwise to release water. Remove and clean the filter of all debris.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose for kinks
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer away from the wall and inspect the entire length of the drain hose for kinks, sharp bends, or compression. The hose should have a smooth path with no sharp turns.

fixSteps.title: Verify drain hose height
fixSteps.instruction: The drain hose should feed into a standpipe or sink drain at a height between 24 and 96 inches from the floor. Too high and the pump cannot push water up. Too low and water may siphon back in.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the drain pump and test the motor for continuity (5-15 ohms expected). Check that the impeller spins freely without grinding. Replace the pump if it fails either test.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the filter and checking the hose does not resolve the issue, the drain pump may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer dr drain error spin cycle fix

faq.q: How is dr different from OE?
faq.a: Both relate to drainage, but dr occurs specifically when the washer cannot drain enough water to begin the spin cycle. OE is a more general drain failure at any point in the cycle.

faq.q: Why does my washer drain sometimes but show dr at spin?
faq.a: The spin cycle requires faster and more complete drainage than the wash cycle. A partial blockage may allow enough flow during wash but not during the higher-demand spin drain.

faq.q: Can the drain hose height cause dr?
faq.a: Yes, if the standpipe or drain connection is higher than 96 inches, the pump may struggle to push water up to that height. The ideal range is 24-96 inches.

title: Network Error (Smart ThinQ)
shortDescription: The washer's Wi-Fi module or smart connectivity feature has an error.
fullDescription: The nE error code indicates the washer's built-in Wi-Fi module or network connectivity is experiencing an issue. This is specific to LG Smart ThinQ-enabled washers and relates to the wireless communication feature, not the washer's core operation. The washer can still be operated normally using the physical control panel, but remote monitoring and smart features will not work.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi module failed to initialize
Router or network configuration change
Faulty Wi-Fi module
Software glitch in the communication processor

symptoms: nE displayed briefly on the control panel
Cannot connect to the LG ThinQ app
Wi-Fi indicator is not lit
Smart diagnosis feature does not work

fixSteps.title: Reset the Wi-Fi connection
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Wi-Fi button on the washer control panel for 3 seconds to reset the wireless module. Wait for the Wi-Fi indicator to flash, indicating it is searching for a network.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 2 minutes and plug it back in. This resets all electronic modules including the Wi-Fi chip. Then reconnect to your network through the LG ThinQ app.

fixSteps.title: Check your Wi-Fi network
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your Wi-Fi router is working and that other devices can connect. The washer requires a 2.4 GHz network (not 5 GHz). Check that the router is within range and not overloaded with connected devices.

fixSteps.title: Re-register the washer in ThinQ
fixSteps.instruction: Delete the washer from your LG ThinQ app. Factory reset the washer's Wi-Fi by pressing and holding the Wi-Fi and Temp buttons simultaneously for 3 seconds. Then follow the app's setup process to re-register the washer.

whenToCallPro: Only if the Wi-Fi module has physically failed and needs replacement. The washer operates normally without Wi-Fi.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer nE network error WiFi ThinQ fix

faq.q: Can I still wash clothes with an nE error?
faq.a: Yes, nE only affects the smart connectivity features. You can use the washer normally with the physical controls on the control panel.

faq.q: Does my LG washer need Wi-Fi to operate?
faq.a: No, Wi-Fi is optional and only used for remote monitoring, smart diagnosis, and app control. The washer works fully without a network connection.

faq.q: Why does my washer only connect to 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Most LG smart appliances use 2.4 GHz because it has better range and wall penetration than 5 GHz. If your router broadcasts both frequencies with the same network name, try creating a separate 2.4 GHz network.

title: Door Unlock Error
shortDescription: The washer door lock mechanism is unable to unlock the door.
fullDescription: The Ed error code indicates the washer is unable to unlock the door after a cycle has completed. This is the opposite of the dE error, which means the door cannot lock. The door lock actuator may have failed, the lock mechanism may be jammed, or there could be a control board communication issue preventing the unlock signal.

causes.cause: Door lock actuator stuck in the locked position
Temporary control board glitch preventing unlock signal
Broken door lock mechanism
Wiring harness issue between control board and door lock

symptoms: Ed displayed after cycle completion
Door will not open
Clothes are trapped inside the drum
Door lock makes clicking sounds but does not release

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: After a cycle completes, the door may take 1-3 minutes to unlock. Wait patiently and try again. If the washer was running a hot cycle, it may need to cool down before unlocking.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle to release the lock
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the washer off using the Power button. Unplug it from the wall for 5 minutes. Plug it back in and turn it on. Try opening the door. The reset may release the lock mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Use the emergency drain cord
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small access panel at the front bottom of the washer. Some LG models have a manual door release pull tab (usually orange or red) next to the drain pump filter. Pull this tab to manually release the door lock.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the lock mechanism has physically failed, disconnect power. Peel back the door boot seal and remove the door lock assembly (two Torx screws). Disconnect the wiring harness and install a new lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot open the door using the emergency release and need to retrieve clothes urgently, or if the lock assembly needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer Ed error door stuck won't open fix

faq.q: My clothes are trapped. How do I open the door?
faq.a: Try unplugging the washer for 5 minutes first. If that does not work, use the manual emergency door release tab found behind the small access panel at the front bottom of the washer.

faq.q: Why does the door stay locked after the cycle?
faq.a: A safety delay of 1-3 minutes after the cycle is normal. The door lock may also fail due to a worn actuator or control board glitch. Power cycling usually resolves intermittent issues.

faq.q: Can I use a credit card to open the stuck door?
faq.a: This is not recommended as it can damage the door seal and lock mechanism. Use the built-in emergency door release instead, which is designed for this exact situation.

title: Door Lock Mechanism Failure
shortDescription: The physical door lock mechanism has failed to engage properly.
fullDescription: The dE1 error code is a more specific variant of the dE error, indicating the door lock's mechanical latch has failed to engage. While dE can be caused by the door not being closed, dE1 specifically points to the lock mechanism itself. The lock solenoid or actuator inside the door lock assembly may be worn, broken, or electrically failed.

causes.cause: Worn or broken door lock actuator
Door lock solenoid coil failure
Misaligned door strike preventing proper engagement
Electrical failure in the lock circuit wiring

symptoms: dE1 displayed on the control panel
Door closes but lock does not click or engage
Washer will not start any cycle
Lock attempts to engage (clicking sound) but fails

fixSteps.title: Inspect and clean the door strike
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the hook on the door that inserts into the lock. Clean any lint or debris from the strike and the lock receiver. Ensure the strike is straight and not bent.

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes. This resets the door lock actuator. Plug it back in, close the door firmly, and attempt to start a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the door lock assembly by peeling back the door seal. Disconnect the wiring harness and test each solenoid coil with a multimeter. Each should show continuity (typically 200-600 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the two Torx screws securing the lock assembly. Pull it out through the front of the washer. Install the new assembly, secure with screws, reconnect the wiring harness, and reseat the door seal.

whenToCallPro: If the wiring harness between the lock and control board is damaged, professional repair is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer dE1 door lock replacement

faq.q: Is dE1 different from dE?
faq.a: Yes. dE is a general door error that can mean the door is not closed. dE1 specifically indicates the lock mechanism itself has failed to engage, even when the door is properly closed.

faq.q: How long does a door lock assembly last?
faq.a: Door lock assemblies typically last 5-10 years depending on usage frequency. They are one of the more commonly replaced parts on front-load washers.

faq.q: Can I temporarily fix the lock?
faq.a: No, there is no safe temporary fix for a door lock failure. The lock must function properly for the washer to operate safely. Replacement is the correct solution.

title: Door Switch Circuit Error
shortDescription: The door switch feedback circuit is not reporting properly to the control board.
fullDescription: The dE2 error code indicates the door switch circuit is not providing the expected feedback to the main control board. Even if the door is closed and the lock engages, the switch that confirms the locked state to the control board is not making proper contact. This is a circuit-level issue rather than a mechanical lock failure.

causes.cause: Faulty door switch contacts inside the lock assembly
Corroded or damaged wiring between door switch and control board
Loose connector at the control board end
Main control board door switch input circuit failure

symptoms: dE2 displayed on the control panel
Door closes and locks but washer thinks it is open
Washer will not start despite door being securely closed
Intermittent error that comes and goes

fixSteps.title: Cycle the door multiple times
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the washer door firmly 5-6 times. This can clean the switch contacts if they are slightly oxidized. Then attempt to start a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel. Follow the door lock wiring harness to the main control board and ensure the connector is firmly seated. Disconnect and reconnect it to clean the contacts.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the door lock wiring from the control board. With a multimeter, test the switch pins for continuity when the door is closed and locked (manually engage the lock by inserting a flat tool into the strike opening). The switch should show continuity when locked.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch contacts are faulty, the entire door lock assembly needs replacement since the switch is integrated. Remove the two Torx screws, disconnect the harness, and install the new assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the wiring harness has damage or if you suspect the control board's switch input circuit has failed.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer dE2 door switch error fix

faq.q: Why does dE2 happen intermittently?
faq.a: Intermittent dE2 usually indicates oxidized or worn switch contacts inside the lock assembly. The contact may work sometimes but not consistently, causing the error to come and go.

faq.q: Is dE2 the same repair as dE1?
faq.a: Often yes, since both are resolved by replacing the door lock assembly. However, dE2 can also be caused by wiring issues between the lock and control board, which dE1 typically is not.

faq.q: Can I test the door switch without removing the lock?
faq.a: Yes, you can test at the control board end by disconnecting the wiring harness and testing for continuity on the switch pins while manually engaging the lock through the door opening.

title: Water Inlet Error (Alternate Display)
shortDescription: Alternate display of the IE water inlet error code on some LG models.
fullDescription: The 1E code is the same as IE (water inlet error) but may appear differently on certain LG washer displays where the number 1 and the letter I look identical. This error indicates the washer is not filling with water within the expected timeframe. All causes, diagnostics, and repairs are identical to the IE error code.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets are turned off or not fully open
Clogged inlet hose screens or filters
Kinked water inlet hoses
Defective water inlet valve

symptoms: 1E or IE displayed on the control panel
Washer does not fill with water
Washer fills extremely slowly
Error appears at the start of a cycle

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply valves
fixSteps.instruction: Check both hot and cold water faucets behind the washer are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply and disconnect inlet hoses. Check for kinks, blockages, or damage. Straighten or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the mesh screens at the washer inlet connections and inside the hose fittings. Rinse under running water.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the inlet valve and test each solenoid coil with a multimeter for 500-1500 ohms resistance. Replace if out of range.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve needs replacement or if water pressure is consistently too low.
videoSearchQuery: LG washer IE 1E error water inlet fix

faq.q: Is 1E the same as IE?
faq.a: Yes, 1E and IE are the same error code. The display may show it as 1E on some models where the number 1 and letter I appear identical on the LED segment display.

faq.q: Why does only hot or cold water fill?
faq.a: If only one temperature fills, the issue is on the other side: either the faucet is off, the screen is clogged, or that side of the dual inlet valve has failed.

faq.q: How much water pressure does the washer need?
faq.a: LG washers require a minimum of 20 PSI water pressure. Most homes have 40-80 PSI, which is well within range.
